item.description:
## DuoUpdater 0.1.4
Fewer missed updates, more one-click installs, and a smoother update flow.
**Updates** - Fixed updates being missed for apps whose download feed is cached very aggressively by the vendor (Fork was a clear case — a new version could go undetected indefinitely). Feeds are now always revalidated, so new releases show up right away. - More apps can now be updated with one click. Apps that ship an unsigned Sparkle feed (like Fork) are now installed directly — verified by the downloaded app's Developer ID signature and matching team, the same safety check used for direct-download and GitHub apps.
**After updating** - A running app is now restarted for you automatically once its update is installed, so a single "Update" click finishes the job — no separate "Restart" step. The app is asked to quit normally, so unsaved-work prompts still appear and anything that won't quit simply keeps its "Restart" button. You can turn this off in Settings.
**Workbench** - Right-click any app for quick "Open" and "Changelog" actions — "Changelog" jumps straight to that app's release notes in the workbench.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.3
Reliability and safety fixes.
**Updates** - App Store updates now run one at a time, so triggering several Mac App Store updates at once no longer lets two automations fight over the single App Store window. - TestFlight badges no longer occasionally stay missing when you open the app during a background check. - Homebrew formula release notes no longer double-fetch on a fast click (less chance of hitting GitHub's rate limit). - A completed install can no longer briefly "stick" on a stale progress state.
**Install safety** - Rollback now refuses to restore a backup whose code signature has been corrupted or tampered with, instead of swapping the broken bundle over your working app. - Recovering from an interrupted update now verifies the bundle's signature before restoring it. - For a disk image that contains several installers from one vendor, package updates now pick the one that matches the app being updated.
**Workbench window** - Opening the window (or the welcome/setup screen) now reliably brings it to the front when it was already open behind another window. - Opening a second window no longer steals focus unnecessarily. - Large release notes render without hitching, and the changelog rail no longer re-measures on every interaction.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.2
- Released: 2026-06-20 - Build: 3 - Package: notarized macOS app bundle - Install: download `DuoUpdater-0.1.2-macos.zip`, unzip it, move `DuoUpdater.app` to `/Applications`, then open it once.
SHA-256: `60e59ce6d78dc9e06d22dfacf724adad14e3c3500b22bdf474a5732c5a37d92c`
